title: Cannabis for Anxiety Is One of the Most Widespread Medical Beliefs of Our Time. The Evidence Just Collapsed.
description: A Lancet Psychiatry review of 54 randomized controlled trials — the largest synthesis of medicinal cannabis mental health data ever conducted — found no clinically meaningful evidence that cannabis treats anxiety, PTSD, or psychosis. More troubling: depression was never even studied in RCTs, meaning the belief that cannabis treats it has no controlled trial basis whatsoever. Here's what the science actually says, and why it got buried under a decade of cultural momentum.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Cannabis for Anxiety Is One of the Most Widespread Medical Beliefs of Our Time. The Evidence Just Collapsed.
